**[I. Purpose and Scope]{.underline}**

This memorandum summarizes the independent appraisal of the Total Enterprise Value (\"TEV\") of Meridian Industrial Holdings, Inc. (\"Meridian\" or the \"Company\"), a Delaware corporation with its principal place of business at 4200 Tryon Industrial Parkway, Charlotte, NC 28217 (EIN: 84-3927561). The appraisal was performed by the Valuation Advisory Group of Cartwright National Bank, N.A. (\"Cartwright\" or the \"Bank\"), applying methodologies and procedures consistent with the Bank\'s internal valuation policies and generally accepted valuation principles.

The appraisal was commissioned by Cartwright in its capacity as Administrative Agent for the proposed \$475,000,000 First Lien Credit Facility (the \"First Lien Facility\"), in connection with the Company\'s refinancing of its existing senior secured credit facility, which matures on August 15, 2025. The refinancing transaction contemplates a new capital structure consisting of the First Lien Facility and a \$150,000,000 Second Lien Term Loan B (the \"Second Lien Facility\" and, together with the First Lien Facility, the \"Credit Facilities\"). The Second Lien Facility is being arranged by Ridgeline Capital Partners, LP (\"Ridgeline\"), acting as Second Lien Agent.

The valuation is intended for use by the first lien lending syndicate organized by Cartwright and, as applicable, the second lien lending group led by Ridgeline, for the following purposes: (a) underwriting and credit approval of the proposed Credit Facilities; (b) assessment of loan-to-value ratios and credit metrics in connection with internal risk rating and regulatory compliance; and (c) intercreditor negotiations between the first lien and second lien lending groups, including the establishment of appropriate equity cushion thresholds under the proposed Intercreditor Agreement.

The valuation date for purposes of this appraisal is May 15, 2025 (the \"Valuation Date\"). All financial data, market observations, and comparable company metrics utilized in this analysis are as of or for the periods ending on or about the Valuation Date, unless otherwise noted. The Company\'s financial results are presented on a last-twelve-months (\"LTM\") basis through the fiscal quarter ended March 31, 2025.

Meridian Industrial Holdings, Inc. is a diversified industrial manufacturer and services provider operating across multiple end markets, including general industrial, infrastructure, and energy-related sectors. The Company operates through a network of manufacturing and service facilities located primarily in the southeastern and midwestern United States. As of the Valuation Date, Meridian employs approximately 2,400 full-time equivalent employees and generates revenue through a combination of engineered products, aftermarket parts and services, and long-term maintenance contracts.

**[II. Conclusion of Value]{.underline}**

Based upon the analyses described herein, the Valuation Advisory Group of Cartwright National Bank, N.A. has concluded that the appraised Total Enterprise Value of Meridian Industrial Holdings, Inc. is approximately **\$640,000,000** (six hundred forty million dollars) as of the Valuation Date of May 15, 2025.

This figure represents the midpoint of a valuation range of **\$620,000,000 to \$660,000,000**. The concluded TEV is derived from a blended analysis that weights the two primary valuation methodologies as follows:

> • **Discounted Cash Flow (\"DCF\") Analysis:** weighted at **50%**, yielding an indicated TEV range of \$615,000,000 to \$655,000,000; and
>
> • **Comparable Company Trading Multiples Analysis:** weighted at **50%**, yielding an indicated TEV range (after adjustments) of \$620,000,000 to \$660,000,000.

The equal weighting of these two methodologies reflects the Valuation Advisory Group\'s assessment that both approaches provide meaningful and reliable indications of value for a company of Meridian\'s profile. The DCF analysis captures the Company\'s projected cash flow generation capacity and forward-looking operating trajectory, while the comparable company analysis anchors the valuation in observable market data reflecting current investor expectations for similarly situated industrial businesses.

The concluded TEV of \$640,000,000 implies the following credit metrics based on the proposed capital structure of the Credit Facilities:

  ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  **Metric**                         **Calculation**                                            **Result**
  ---------------------------------- ---------------------------------------------------------- -----------------------
  Total Secured Debt                 \$475,000,000 (First Lien) + \$150,000,000 (Second Lien)   \$625,000,000

  Total Loan-to-Value (\"LTV\")      \$625,000,000 ÷ \$640,000,000                              **97.66%**

  First Lien LTV                     \$475,000,000 ÷ \$640,000,000                              **74.22%**

  Second Lien Equity Cushion         (\$640,000,000 − \$475,000,000) ÷ \$640,000,000            **25.78%**

  First Lien Leverage (LTM EBITDA)   \$475,000,000 ÷ \$89,400,000                               **5.31x**

  Total Leverage (LTM EBITDA)        \$625,000,000 ÷ \$89,400,000                               **6.99x**
  ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The Total LTV of approximately 97.66% indicates that the aggregate amount of first lien and second lien secured debt represents substantially all of the concluded TEV, leaving limited residual equity value beneath the second lien tranche. The First Lien LTV of approximately 74.22% provides meaningful asset coverage for the first lien lending syndicate, with approximately \$165,000,000 of implied enterprise value in excess of the First Lien Facility. The Second Lien equity cushion of approximately 25.78% represents the percentage of TEV available to absorb value deterioration before the first lien lenders\' recovery is impaired, and is a key metric for the intercreditor negotiations between Cartwright and Ridgeline.

The leverage multiples noted above---5.31x for the First Lien Facility and 6.99x on a total basis---are consistent with leveraged credit transactions in the industrial sector, though they reflect a capital structure that is positioned at the upper end of the range for companies with Meridian\'s operating profile and margin characteristics. These metrics should be evaluated in the context of the Company\'s cash flow generation capacity, capital expenditure requirements, and debt service coverage ratios as detailed in the credit memorandum prepared separately by Cartwright\'s Leveraged Finance Group.

**[III. Discounted Cash Flow Analysis]{.underline}**

The Valuation Advisory Group performed a discounted cash flow analysis based on a five-year projection period spanning the fiscal years ending December 31, 2025 through December 31, 2029 (the \"Projection Period\"). The projections utilized in the DCF analysis are based on Meridian management\'s business plan and financial forecasts, as furnished to Cartwright in connection with the refinancing, and as adjusted by the Valuation Advisory Group to reflect the Bank\'s independent assessment of achievability, end-market conditions, and macroeconomic assumptions. The adjustments made by the Valuation Advisory Group were informed by consultations with Cartwright\'s industry specialists in the industrial and manufacturing sectors, as well as publicly available market data and third-party research.

The key assumptions underlying the DCF analysis are summarized below:

**Revenue Growth.** Projected revenue is assumed to grow at a compound annual growth rate (\"CAGR\") of approximately **4.0% to 5.5%** over the Projection Period. This growth assumption reflects the Company\'s current backlog visibility, which as of the Valuation Date stood at approximately \$285,000,000 representing roughly 5.5 months of trailing revenue, as well as management\'s identified pipeline of new customer engagements and contract renewals. The growth rate also takes into account end-market conditions in the industrial sector, including modest expansion in non-residential construction activity, sustained infrastructure spending driven by federal appropriations, and stable demand trends in the energy-related maintenance and services segments. The Valuation Advisory Group applied a modestly conservative adjustment to the upper end of management\'s revenue forecast to account for execution risk associated with certain new product introductions and geographic expansion initiatives planned for fiscal years 2027 and 2028.

**EBITDA Margins.** Projected EBITDA margins are assumed to stabilize in the range of **14.5% to 16.0%** over the Projection Period, consistent with the Company\'s recent operating performance. The Company\'s LTM EBITDA margin as of the Valuation Date was approximately 14.8%, calculated on LTM EBITDA of \$89,400,000 and implied LTM revenue of approximately \$604,100,000. Management\'s projections assume modest margin improvement over the Projection Period driven by operating leverage on incremental revenue, procurement savings from recently renegotiated supply agreements, and the expected contribution of higher-margin aftermarket and services revenue as a proportion of the total revenue mix. The Valuation Advisory Group accepted management\'s margin assumptions at the lower end of the projected range and applied a haircut to the upper-end scenario reflecting potential headwinds from labor cost inflation and raw material price volatility, both of which have exhibited heightened uncertainty in the current macroeconomic environment.

**Discount Rate / WACC.** The projected free cash flows were discounted to present value using a weighted average cost of capital (\"WACC\") in the range of **10.5% to 11.5%**. The WACC was derived using a build-up methodology incorporating the following components:

> • Risk-free rate based on the 20-year U.S. Treasury yield of approximately 4.85% as of the analysis date;
>
> • Equity risk premium of 5.50%, based on the Duff & Phelps (Kroll) recommended equity risk premium as of the first quarter of 2025;
>
> • Size premium of 1.75%, reflecting Meridian\'s positioning as a mid-market industrial company with a TEV below \$1 billion;
>
> • Company-specific risk premium of 1.00% to 1.50%, reflecting execution risk associated with the Company\'s growth initiatives, customer concentration considerations, and the leveraged nature of the proposed capital structure;
>
> • Pre-tax cost of debt of approximately 8.25% to 8.75%, reflecting the anticipated pricing on the proposed Credit Facilities (SOFR + 300 to 350 basis points on the First Lien Facility), with the Secured Overnight Financing Rate (\"SOFR\") at 5.33% as of the analysis date; and
>
> • Target capital structure weighting of 60% debt / 40% equity, based on the proposed capital structure and observed capital structures of comparable companies in the industrial sector.

The resulting WACC range of 10.5% to 11.5% is consistent with discount rates applied in recent leveraged lending appraisals for similarly situated industrial companies within Cartwright\'s portfolio and in the broader leveraged finance market.

**Terminal Value.** The terminal value, representing the estimated enterprise value of Meridian at the end of the Projection Period, was calculated using a **perpetuity growth method** with a long-term growth rate of **2.5%**. This long-term growth rate is intended to approximate the expected long-run nominal growth rate of the U.S. economy and the Company\'s addressable end markets, and is within the range generally accepted in valuation practice for mature industrial businesses.

As a reasonableness check, the terminal value derived from the perpetuity growth method was cross-referenced against a terminal value implied by an **EV/EBITDA exit multiple of 7.0x to 7.5x** applied to the projected terminal year (2029) EBITDA. The two approaches yielded terminal value indications within an acceptable convergence range, providing the Valuation Advisory Group with confidence in the reasonableness of the terminal value assumptions. The exit multiple range of 7.0x to 7.5x is consistent with the current trading multiples observed for the comparable company peer group, as discussed in Section IV below, and reflects a conservative assumption that the Company\'s exit valuation will not benefit from meaningful multiple expansion relative to current market levels.

**DCF-Indicated TEV Range.** The discounted cash flow analysis, incorporating the assumptions described above and reflecting the present value of projected unlevered free cash flows during the Projection Period plus the present value of the terminal value, yields an indicated Total Enterprise Value range of **\$615,000,000 to \$655,000,000**. The midpoint of this range is approximately \$635,000,000.

The sensitivity of the DCF valuation to key assumptions was tested across a matrix of discount rates and terminal growth rates. Under the base case assumptions (WACC of 11.0% and terminal growth rate of 2.5%), the DCF-indicated TEV is approximately \$635,000,000. A 50 basis point increase in the discount rate, holding all other assumptions constant, reduces the indicated TEV by approximately \$25,000,000 to \$30,000,000, while a 50 basis point decrease in the terminal growth rate reduces the indicated TEV by approximately \$15,000,000 to \$20,000,000. These sensitivities underscore the importance of the discount rate and terminal value assumptions to the concluded valuation and should be considered by the lending syndicate in evaluating downside scenarios.

**[IV. Comparable Company Analysis]{.underline}**

The Valuation Advisory Group selected a peer group of publicly traded industrial companies with operating characteristics, end-market exposure, revenue scale, and margin profiles broadly comparable to those of Meridian. The selected comparable companies operate in the diversified industrials, specialty manufacturing, and industrial services subsectors, and were chosen based on the following criteria: (a) revenue in the range of approximately \$300,000,000 to \$1,500,000,000; (b) EBITDA margins in the range of approximately 12.0% to 20.0%; (c) meaningful exposure to infrastructure, general industrial, and/or energy-related end markets; and (d) publicly available financial data as of or near the Valuation Date. Companies with significant non-industrial business segments, those undergoing material restructurings, and those subject to pending change-of-control transactions were excluded from the peer group to avoid distortive effects on observed trading multiples.

The key observations from the comparable company analysis are as follows:

**Median EV/LTM EBITDA Multiple.** The selected comparable companies exhibited a **median EV/LTM EBITDA multiple of 7.0x to 7.5x** as of the analysis date. The range reflects variability within the peer group based on differences in growth profiles, margin quality, geographic diversification, and business mix. The interquartile range of observed multiples was approximately 6.5x to 8.2x, indicating a moderately dispersed dataset. The Valuation Advisory Group focused on the median range, rather than the mean, to minimize the impact of outlier observations at either end of the distribution.

**Application to Meridian\'s LTM EBITDA.** The selected multiple range of 7.0x to 7.5x was applied to Meridian\'s LTM EBITDA of **\$89,400,000**, yielding an **unadjusted indicated TEV range of approximately \$625,800,000 to \$670,500,000**. This range represents the implied enterprise value of Meridian if it were valued at the median trading multiples of the selected peer group, before consideration of any company-specific adjustments.

**Adjustments.** The Valuation Advisory Group applied the following adjustments to the unadjusted indicated TEV range to arrive at a company-specific valuation indication:

> • *Size Discount:* A modest downward adjustment was applied to reflect the fact that Meridian, as a privately held middle-market company, is smaller in scale and has a less diversified revenue base than many of the publicly traded comparables in the peer group. Smaller companies generally trade at lower multiples due to higher perceived risk, more limited access to capital markets, and greater vulnerability to customer and supplier concentration.
>
> • *Liquidity Considerations:* An additional adjustment was made to account for the lack of public market liquidity for Meridian\'s equity interests. While the lender appraisal is conducted on an enterprise value basis rather than an equity value basis, the absence of a liquid public market for the Company\'s securities is a relevant consideration in assessing appropriate valuation multiples, as public company multiples inherently reflect the liquidity premium associated with publicly traded securities.
>
> • *Company-Specific Risk Factors:* The Valuation Advisory Group considered certain company-specific risk factors that may warrant a valuation adjustment relative to the peer group median, including the Company\'s customer concentration profile (with the top five customers representing approximately 32% of LTM revenue), geographic concentration in the southeastern United States, and the capital-intensive nature of certain of the Company\'s manufacturing operations, which may constrain free cash flow conversion relative to asset-lighter peer companies.

After application of these adjustments, the **comparable company-indicated TEV range (adjusted) is \$620,000,000 to \$660,000,000**. The midpoint of this adjusted range is \$640,000,000, which is consistent with the midpoint indicated by the DCF analysis and provides corroborative support for the concluded TEV.

The Valuation Advisory Group notes that the comparable company analysis is inherently limited by the availability of truly comparable publicly traded companies and by the fact that public market valuations can be influenced by factors not directly relevant to a private company lender appraisal, including public market sentiment, index inclusion effects, and trading volume dynamics. Accordingly, the comparable company analysis is used in conjunction with, rather than as a substitute for, the DCF analysis in arriving at the concluded TEV.

**[V. Key Assumptions and Limiting Conditions]{.underline}**

The appraisal summarized herein is subject to the following key assumptions, qualifications, and limiting conditions, which should be carefully reviewed by all recipients of this memorandum:

**Reliance on Company-Provided Information.** The appraisal relies upon financial statements, management projections, operational data, asset schedules, backlog reports, and other information furnished by Meridian management and reviewed by Cartwright\'s Valuation Advisory Group and Leveraged Finance Group. These materials have not been independently audited or verified for purposes of this analysis. The Valuation Advisory Group has assumed, without independent verification, the accuracy and completeness of all information provided by the Company. While the Valuation Advisory Group has performed reasonableness checks on key financial data points and has made certain adjustments to management\'s projections as described herein, the reliability of the concluded valuation is dependent upon the accuracy and completeness of the underlying information furnished by the Company.

**LTM EBITDA.** The valuation relies upon a last-twelve-months EBITDA figure of **\$89,400,000** as reported by the Company for the twelve-month period ended March 31, 2025. This figure has been reviewed by Cartwright\'s Leveraged Finance Group for consistency with the Company\'s audited financial statements for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2024 and the unaudited financial statements for the quarter ended March 31, 2025. EBITDA as used in this analysis represents earnings before interest expense, income taxes, depreciation, and amortization, and may include certain adjustments for non-recurring items as agreed between the Company and Cartwright; however, the definition of EBITDA applied in this appraisal may differ from the definition of \"Consolidated EBITDA\" or \"Adjusted EBITDA\" as defined in the credit agreement for the proposed Credit Facilities.

**Going-Concern Basis.** The valuation assumes a **going-concern basis** and reflects the estimated value of Meridian as a continuing operating enterprise. The concluded TEV does not reflect liquidation value, forced-sale value, or orderly wind-down value, and should not be interpreted as an indication of the proceeds that would be realized in a distressed sale, bankruptcy proceeding, or other forced disposition of the Company\'s assets. The going-concern assumption presumes that the Company will continue to operate in the ordinary course, will have access to adequate working capital and liquidity, and will not be subject to any material adverse change in its business, financial condition, or regulatory environment that would fundamentally impair its ability to operate.

**No Physical Inspection.** No physical inspection of the Company\'s real property, manufacturing facilities, equipment, or other tangible assets was conducted in connection with this appraisal. The analysis relies on asset schedules, depreciation records, and capital expenditure histories provided by the Company, supplemented by publicly available information regarding comparable industrial properties and equipment. The Valuation Advisory Group makes no representation regarding the physical condition, environmental compliance, or replacement cost of the Company\'s assets.

**Not a Fairness Opinion.** This summary memorandum does not constitute a fairness opinion and should not be construed as providing any opinion or recommendation as to the fairness, from a financial point of view, of the terms of the proposed Credit Facilities or any other transaction involving the Company. The purpose of this appraisal is solely to provide an independent estimate of TEV for use in connection with credit underwriting, risk assessment, and intercreditor analysis, and does not address the adequacy of consideration, the merits of the proposed transaction, or the financial interests of any particular party.

**Subject to Change.** The valuation presented herein is based on conditions existing as of the Valuation Date of May 15, 2025, and is subject to change based on, among other things, subsequent movements in market conditions, changes in the interest rate environment (including changes in the SOFR reference rate), fluctuations in public equity market valuations and comparable company trading multiples, updated financial performance of the Company, changes in the Company\'s competitive position or end-market conditions, and any material events or developments occurring after the Valuation Date. The Valuation Advisory Group undertakes no obligation to update or revise this appraisal for events or circumstances arising after the Valuation Date, unless separately engaged to do so.

**Debt-Free, Cash-Free Basis.** The concluded TEV is presented on a **debt-free, cash-free basis**, representing the value of the Company\'s total invested capital without regard to the Company\'s existing or proposed capital structure, cash balances, or net working capital adjustments. The conversion of TEV to equity value, if applicable, would require adjustments for outstanding indebtedness, cash and cash equivalents, and other balance sheet items, which are beyond the scope of this appraisal.

**Governing Law.** The engagement terms under which this appraisal was performed, including confidentiality obligations, limitation of liability provisions, and dispute resolution mechanisms, are governed by the laws of the State of **New York**, without regard to its conflicts of law principles.

**[VI. Distribution and Use]{.underline}**

This summary memorandum is provided solely for the use of Cartwright National Bank, N.A., in its capacity as Administrative Agent under the proposed First Lien Facility, and the syndicate of first lien lenders participating in the First Lien Facility. In addition, this memorandum is made available to Ridgeline Capital Partners, LP, in its capacity as Second Lien Agent under the proposed Second Lien Facility, and the participating second lien lenders, in each case solely in connection with the proposed Credit Facilities and the intercreditor arrangements being negotiated between the first lien and second lien lending groups for Meridian Industrial Holdings, Inc.

This memorandum may not be used for any purpose other than the evaluation, underwriting, credit approval, and administration of the proposed Credit Facilities and the related intercreditor negotiations. Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, this memorandum may not be used in connection with any public offering, securities filing, regulatory submission, litigation proceeding, or other purpose without the prior written consent of Cartwright National Bank, N.A.

Redistribution, reproduction, or dissemination of this memorandum, in whole or in part, to any person or entity other than the intended recipients described above, is strictly prohibited without the prior written consent of Cartwright National Bank, N.A. Each recipient of this memorandum agrees, by accepting delivery hereof, to be bound by the foregoing restrictions on distribution and use.

The Valuation Advisory Group of Cartwright National Bank, N.A. expressly disclaims any liability to any person or entity other than the intended recipients of this memorandum for any losses, damages, or claims arising out of or in connection with the use of, or reliance upon, the information, analyses, or conclusions contained herein.

\"**Consolidated EBITDA**\" means, for any period, Consolidated Net Income for such period *plus*, without duplication and to the extent deducted (and not added back) in calculating Consolidated Net Income for such period, the sum of (a) consolidated interest expense, (b) provision for federal, state, local, and foreign income taxes, (c) depreciation and amortization expense, (d) non-cash charges (including non-cash equity-based compensation expense), (e) fees, costs, and expenses incurred in connection with the Transactions, including one-time closing costs and professional fees, (f) fees, costs, and expenses incurred in connection with any Permitted Acquisition, Investment, disposition, recapitalization, or issuance of Indebtedness permitted hereunder (whether or not consummated), (g) restructuring and integration charges and related business optimization expenses (including facility consolidation costs, retention and severance costs, contract termination costs, future lease commitments, and costs related to entry into new markets), not to exceed 15% of Consolidated EBITDA for such period (calculated before giving effect to any add-back pursuant to this clause (g)), and (h) losses from Asset Dispositions (other than Asset Dispositions in the ordinary course of business), *minus*, without duplication and to the extent included in calculating Consolidated Net Income for such period, (i) non-cash gains increasing Consolidated Net Income for such period (excluding any non-cash gain to the extent it represents the reversal of an accrual or cash reserve for a potential cash item that reduced Consolidated EBITDA in any prior period) and (ii) gains from Asset Dispositions (other than Asset Dispositions in the ordinary course of business).

\"**Consolidated Net Income**\" means, for any period, the net income (or net loss) of the Borrower and its Restricted Subsidiaries for such period determined on a consolidated basis in accordance with GAAP; *provided* that there shall be excluded (a) the net income (or net loss) of any Person that is not a Restricted Subsidiary, except to the extent of the amount of dividends or similar distributions actually paid in cash (or to the extent converted into cash) to the Borrower or a Restricted Subsidiary by such Person during such period, (b) the net income (or net loss) of any Person accrued prior to the date it becomes a Restricted Subsidiary or is merged into or consolidated with the Borrower or any Restricted Subsidiary, (c) the net income of any Restricted Subsidiary to the extent that the declaration or payment of dividends or similar distributions by such Restricted Subsidiary is not at the date of determination permitted without any prior governmental approval (that has not been obtained) or, directly or indirectly, by operation of the terms of its charter or any agreement, instrument, judgment, decree, order, statute, rule, or governmental regulation applicable to that Restricted Subsidiary (other than restrictions under the Loan Documents), and (d) any after-tax gains or losses attributable to Asset Dispositions or returned surplus assets of any Plan.

\"**Consolidated Total Debt**\" means, at any date of determination, the aggregate principal amount of all Indebtedness of the Borrower and its Restricted Subsidiaries outstanding at such date determined on a consolidated basis in accordance with GAAP (but excluding (a) obligations under Hedging Agreements, (b) undrawn letters of credit, and (c) any Indebtedness that has been cash collateralized or defeased).

\"**Debtor Relief Laws**\" means the Bankruptcy Code of the United States of America (Title 11 of the United States Code), and all other liquidation, conservatorship, bankruptcy, assignment for the benefit of creditors, moratorium, rearrangement, receivership, insolvency, reorganization, or similar debtor relief laws of the United States or other applicable jurisdictions from time to time in effect.

\"**Excluded Assets**\" means (a) any fee-owned real property with a fair market value of less than \$2,500,000, (b) motor vehicles and other assets subject to certificates of title to the extent a Lien therein cannot be perfected by filing a UCC financing statement, (c) any Equity Interest in any Excluded Subsidiary, (d) any lease, license, contract, or agreement to which any Loan Party is a party to the extent that a grant of a Lien therein would violate or invalidate such lease, license, contract, or agreement or create a right of termination in favor of any other party thereto (other than to the extent that any such prohibition would be rendered ineffective pursuant to Section 9-406, 9-407, 9-408, or 9-409 of the UCC or any other applicable law or principles of equity), (e) Equity Interests in excess of 65% of the outstanding voting Equity Interests of any Foreign Subsidiary, (f) any governmental licenses or permits that by their terms or by applicable law prohibit the granting of a Lien therein, and (g) any other assets to the extent the Administrative Agent reasonably determines that the costs of obtaining a Lien thereon are excessive in relation to the value to be afforded to the Lenders thereby.

\"**FATCA**\" means Sections 1471 through 1474 of the Code, as of the date of this Agreement (or any amended or successor version that is substantively comparable and not materially more onerous to comply with), any current or future regulations or official interpretations thereof, and any agreements entered into pursuant to Section 1471(b)(1) of the Code, and any fiscal or regulatory legislation, rules, or practices adopted pursuant to any intergovernmental agreement, treaty, or convention among Governmental Authorities and implementing such Sections of the Code.

The amount of any Guarantee shall be deemed to be an amount equal to the stated or determinable amount of the related primary obligation, or portion thereof, in respect of which such Guarantee is made or, if not stated or determinable, the maximum reasonably anticipated liability in respect thereof as determined by the guaranteeing Person in good faith.

For all purposes hereof, the Indebtedness of any Person shall include the Indebtedness of any partnership or joint venture (other than a joint venture that is itself a corporation or limited liability company) in which such Person is a general partner or a joint venturer, unless such Indebtedness is expressly made non-recourse to such Person.

\"**Investment**\" means, as to any Person, any direct or indirect acquisition or investment by such Person, whether by means of (a) the purchase or other acquisition of Equity Interests of another Person, (b) a loan, advance, or capital contribution to, Guarantee or assumption of Indebtedness of, or purchase or other acquisition of any other debt or equity participation or interest in, another Person, or (c) the purchase or other acquisition (in one transaction or a series of transactions) of assets of another Person that constitute a business unit, line of business, division, or all or substantially all of the assets of such Person. For purposes of covenant compliance, the amount of any Investment shall be the amount actually invested, without adjustment for subsequent increases or decreases in the value of such Investment.

For purposes of any Second Lien Purchase Right under the Intercreditor Agreement, LC Exposure shall constitute First Lien Obligations and any purchaser of the First Lien Obligations must either (x) cause the outstanding Letters of Credit to be cash collateralized at 103% of face amount or (y) cause substitute letters of credit to be issued in replacement thereof in form and substance satisfactory to the Issuing Bank, in each case simultaneously with the consummation of any such purchase.

\"**Lender**\" means each financial institution listed on Schedule 2.01 as having a Commitment and each other financial institution that becomes a party hereto pursuant to an Assignment and Assumption or a Joinder Agreement (in the case of an Incremental Facility), other than any such Person that ceases to be a party hereto pursuant to an Assignment and Assumption.

\"**Net Cash Proceeds**\" means (a) with respect to any Asset Disposition or Casualty Event, the cash proceeds (including any cash received by way of deferred payment pursuant to a promissory note, finder\'s fees, and transaction costs paid or payable to Persons who are not Affiliates of the Borrower in connection therewith, (ii) taxes paid or reasonably estimated to be payable in connection therewith (after taking into account any available tax credits or deductions and any tax-sharing arrangements), (iii) amounts required to be applied to repay Indebtedness (other than the Obligations and other than Second Lien Obligations) secured by a Lien on the asset sold, transferred, or otherwise disposed of that is permitted hereunder and that is senior to the Lien securing the Obligations (or, in the case of Indebtedness secured by a Lien of equal priority with the Liens securing the Obligations, the portion of such proceeds required to repay such Indebtedness in accordance with the terms of the applicable intercreditor, pari passu, or similar arrangement), (iv) appropriate amounts to be provided by the Borrower or any Restricted Subsidiary as a reserve, in accordance with GAAP, against any liabilities associated with such Asset Disposition or Casualty Event and retained by the Borrower or any Restricted Subsidiary after such Asset Disposition or Casualty Event, including pension and other post-employment benefit liabilities, liabilities related to environmental matters, and liabilities under any indemnification obligations associated with such Asset Disposition or Casualty Event (provided that the amount of any subsequent reduction of such reserve (other than in connection with a payment in respect of any such liability) shall be deemed to be Net Cash Proceeds of such Asset Disposition or Casualty Event occurring on the date of such reduction), and (v) the principal amount, premium, or penalty, if any, interest, and other amounts on any Indebtedness that is secured by the asset subject to such Asset Disposition or Casualty Event and that is required (other than by this Agreement) to be, and is, repaid in connection therewith; *provided* that Net Cash Proceeds shall be calculated without deduction for any amounts payable to holders of Second Lien Obligations unless and until all First Lien Obligations shall have been indefeasibly paid in full and the Revolving Commitments shall have been terminated, and (b) with respect to any issuance or incurrence of Indebtedness, the cash proceeds therefrom, net of underwriting discounts and commissions, placement agent fees, and other reasonable and customary out-of-pocket fees and expenses paid or payable in connection therewith.

\"**Related Parties**\" means, with respect to any Person, such Person\'s Affiliates and the partners, directors, officers, employees, agents, trustees, administrators, managers, advisors, and representatives of such Person and of such Person\'s Affiliates.

\"**Reporting Threshold**\" means, with respect to any Asset Disposition or Casualty Event, \$3,000,000, being the threshold amount of Net Cash Proceeds above which the Borrower must deliver a Prepayment Event Notice under Section 2.05(b)(v). The Reporting Threshold is set below the \$5,000,000 individual prepayment trigger set forth in Section 2.05(b)(i) to provide the Administrative Agent and the Lenders with early visibility into potential mandatory prepayment events.

\"**Required Lenders**\" means, at any time, Lenders having Revolving Commitments and/or outstanding Term Loans representing more than 50% of the sum of (a) the aggregate Revolving Commitments then in effect (or, after the termination of the Revolving Commitments, the Total Revolving Exposure at such time) and (b) the aggregate outstanding principal amount of the Term Loans at such time; *provided* that the Revolving Commitment and outstanding Term Loans of any Defaulting Lender shall be excluded for purposes of determining Required Lenders at any time.

\"**Responsible Officer**\" means the chief executive officer, president, chief financial officer, treasurer, assistant treasurer, controller, secretary, or assistant secretary of the Borrower or any other officer of the Borrower designated in writing to the Administrative Agent by the chief executive officer or chief financial officer of the Borrower, solely with respect to the delivery of incumbency certificates pursuant to Section 3.01 or specific notices required under this Agreement.

\"**Restricted Payment**\" means any dividend or other distribution (whether in cash, securities, or other property) with respect to any Equity Interest of the Borrower or any Restricted Subsidiary, or any payment (whether in cash, securities, or other property), including any sinking fund or similar deposit, on account of the purchase, redemption, retirement, acquisition, cancellation, or termination of any such Equity Interest, or on account of any return of capital to the Borrower\'s stockholders, partners, or members (or the equivalent Persons thereof).

\"**Restricted Subsidiary**\" means any Subsidiary of the Borrower that is not an Unrestricted Subsidiary.

\"**Revolving Commitment**\" means, with respect to each Revolving Lender, the commitment of such Revolving Lender to make Revolving Loans and to participate in Letters of Credit hereunder, in an aggregate principal amount not to exceed at any time outstanding the amount set forth opposite such Revolving Lender\'s name on Schedule 2.01 under the heading \"Revolving Commitment\" or in the Assignment and Assumption or Joinder Agreement pursuant to which such Lender became a party hereto, as such amount may be adjusted from time to time pursuant to the terms hereof. The aggregate amount of the Revolving Commitments as of the Closing Date is \$125,000,000.

\"**Revolving Loan**\" means a revolving loan made by a Revolving Lender pursuant to Section 2.01(b).

\"**Sale and Leaseback Transaction**\" means any arrangement, directly or indirectly, with any Person whereby the Borrower or a Restricted Subsidiary shall sell or transfer any property, real or personal, used or useful in its business, whether now owned or hereafter acquired, and thereafter rent or lease such property or other property that it intends to use for substantially the same purpose or purposes as the property sold or transferred.

\"**Sanctioned Country**\" means, at any time, a country, region, or territory which is itself, or whose government is, the subject or target of any Sanctions broadly restricting or prohibiting dealings with such country, region, territory, or government (at the time of this Agreement, the so-called Donetsk People\'s Republic, the so-called Luhansk People\'s Republic, the Crimea, Zaporizhzhia, and Kherson regions of Ukraine, Cuba, Iran, North Korea, and Syria).

\"**Sanctioned Person**\" means, at any time, (a) any Person listed in any Sanctions-related list of designated Persons maintained by the Office of Foreign Assets Control of the U.S. Department of the Treasury (\"**OFAC**\"), the U.S. Department of State, the United Nations Security Council, the European Union, any European Union member state, His Majesty\'s Treasury of the United Kingdom, or other relevant sanctions authority, (b) any Person operating, organized, or resident in a Sanctioned Country, (c) any Person owned or controlled by any such Person or Persons described in clauses (a) or (b), or (d) any Person otherwise the subject or target of any Sanctions.

\"**Sanctions**\" means all economic or financial sanctions or trade embargoes imposed, administered, or enforced from time to time by (a) the U.S. government, including those administered by OFAC, the U.S. Department of State, or the U.S. Department of Commerce, (b) the United Nations Security Council, (c) the European Union or any European Union member state, or (d) His Majesty\'s Treasury of the United Kingdom.

\"**SEC**\" means the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission, or any Governmental Authority succeeding to any of its principal functions.

\"**Second Lien Agent**\" means Ridgeline Capital Partners, LP, in its capacity as administrative agent and collateral agent under the Second Lien Credit Agreement and the Second Lien Loan Documents, or any successor agent thereunder.

\"**Second Lien Credit Agreement**\" means that certain Second Lien Credit Agreement, dated as of July 31, 2025, among the Borrower, the lenders party thereto, and Ridgeline Capital Partners, LP, as administrative agent and collateral agent, providing for a second lien term loan B facility in an aggregate principal amount of \$150,000,000, as amended, restated, supplemented, or otherwise modified from time to time in accordance with its terms and the terms hereof and of the Intercreditor Agreement.

\"**Second Lien Loan Documents**\" means the Second Lien Credit Agreement and the other \"Loan Documents\" (as defined in the Second Lien Credit Agreement) entered into in connection therewith.

\"**Second Lien Obligations**\" means all obligations arising under or in connection with the Second Lien Credit Agreement and the Second Lien Loan Documents, including all principal, interest, premium (if any), fees, expenses, indemnities, and other amounts payable thereunder.

\"**Security Agreement**\" means that certain Security Agreement, dated as of July 31, 2025, among the Loan Parties and the Collateral Agent, substantially in the form agreed upon by the Borrower and the Administrative Agent, as amended, restated, supplemented, or otherwise modified from time to time.

\"**Security Documents**\" means the Security Agreement, each Mortgage, and each other agreement, instrument, or document entered into or delivered in connection herewith creating or purporting to create a Lien on any Collateral in favor of the Collateral Agent for the benefit of the Secured Parties.

\"**Similar Business**\" means any business that is the same as or similar, reasonably related, complementary, synergistic, incidental, or ancillary to any of the businesses conducted by the Borrower and its Restricted Subsidiaries on the Closing Date.

\"**SOFR**\" means the Secured Overnight Financing Rate as administered by the Federal Reserve Bank of New York (or a successor administrator of the Secured Overnight Financing Rate).

\"**Solvent**\" means, with respect to any Person and its Subsidiaries on a particular date, that on such date (a) the fair value of the property of such Person and its Subsidiaries on a consolidated basis is greater than the total amount of liabilities, including contingent liabilities, of such Person and its Subsidiaries on a consolidated basis, (b) the present fair saleable value of the assets of such Person and its Subsidiaries on a consolidated basis is not less than the amount that will be required to pay the probable liability of such Person and its Subsidiaries on a consolidated basis on their debts as they become absolute and matured, (c) such Person and its Subsidiaries on a consolidated basis do not intend to, and do not believe that they will, incur debts or liabilities beyond their ability to pay such debts and liabilities as they mature, (d) such Person and its Subsidiaries on a consolidated basis are not engaged in business or a transaction, and are not about to engage in business or a transaction, for which their property would constitute an unreasonably small capital, and (e) such Person and its Subsidiaries on a consolidated basis are able to pay their debts and liabilities, contingent obligations, and other commitments as they mature in the ordinary course of business.

\"**Subsidiary**\" means, with respect to any Person (the \"parent\") at any date of determination, any corporation, limited liability company, partnership, association, or other entity the accounts of which would be consolidated with those of the parent in the parent\'s consolidated financial statements if such financial statements were prepared in accordance with GAAP as of such date, as well as any other corporation, limited liability company, partnership, association, or other entity of which securities or other ownership interests representing more than 50% of the equity or more than 50% of the ordinary voting power or, in the case of a partnership, more than 50% of the general partnership interests are, as of such date, owned, controlled, or held by the parent or one or more subsidiaries of the parent.

\"**Swap Agreement**\" means any agreement with respect to any swap, forward, future, or derivative transaction or option or similar agreement involving, or settled by reference to, one or more rates, currencies, commodities, equity or debt instruments or securities, or economic, financial, or pricing indices or measures of economic, financial, or pricing risk or value, or any similar transaction or any combination of these transactions.

\"**Tax**\" means any present or future tax, levy, impost, duty, assessment, charge, fee, deduction, or withholding of any nature and whatever called, by whomsoever, on whomsoever, and wherever imposed, levied, collected, withheld, or assessed.

\"**Term Loan**\" means a term loan made by a Term Lender pursuant to Section 2.01(a).

\"**Term Loan A Commitment**\" means, with respect to each Term Lender, the commitment of such Term Lender to make Term Loans on the Closing Date in an aggregate principal amount equal to the amount set forth opposite such Term Lender\'s name on Schedule 2.01 under the heading \"Term Loan A Commitment.\" The aggregate Term Loan A Commitments as of the Closing Date are \$350,000,000.

\"**Term SOFR Reference Rate**\" means, for any day and any tenor, the forward-looking term rate based on SOFR that has been selected or recommended by the Relevant Governmental Body and that is published by the CME Group Benchmark Administration Limited (or a successor administrator) for such tenor as of such day.

\"**Total First Lien Facility**\" means the aggregate of the Revolving Credit Facility and the Term Loan A Facility, in an original aggregate principal amount of \$475,000,000.

\"**Total First Lien Net Leverage Ratio**\" means, as of any date of determination, the ratio of (a) Consolidated Total Debt that is secured by a first-priority Lien on the Collateral at such date (including, without limitation, the outstanding principal amount of all Loans and all LC Exposure) *minus* unrestricted cash and Cash Equivalents of the Borrower and the Restricted Subsidiaries at such date (not to exceed \$25,000,000 for purposes of this calculation) to (b) Consolidated EBITDA for the most recently ended four consecutive fiscal quarters for which financial statements have been delivered (or were required to be delivered) pursuant to Section 5.01.

\"**Total Net Leverage Ratio**\" means, as of any date of determination, the ratio of (a) Consolidated Total Debt at such date *minus* unrestricted cash and Cash Equivalents of the Borrower and the Restricted Subsidiaries at such date (not to exceed \$25,000,000 for purposes of this calculation) to (b) Consolidated EBITDA for the most recently ended four consecutive fiscal quarters for which financial statements have been delivered (or were required to be delivered) pursuant to Section 5.01.

\"**Transactions**\" means, collectively, (a) the execution, delivery, and performance by each Loan Party of the Loan Documents to which it is a party, (b) the borrowing of the Loans and the issuance of the Letters of Credit on the Closing Date, (c) the execution, delivery, and performance of the Second Lien Credit Agreement and the related Second Lien Loan Documents, (d) the repayment, discharge, and termination of the Existing Credit Facility, (e) the execution, delivery, and performance of the Intercreditor Agreement, and (f) the payment of fees, costs, and expenses incurred in connection with any of the foregoing.

\"**UCC**\" means the Uniform Commercial Code as in effect from time to time in the State of New York; *provided* that if, by reason of mandatory provisions of law, the attachment, perfection, priority, or the effect of perfection or non-perfection of any Lien on any Collateral is governed by the Uniform Commercial Code as in effect in a jurisdiction other than the State of New York, \"UCC\" means the Uniform Commercial Code as in effect from time to time in such other jurisdiction for purposes of the provisions hereof relating to such attachment, perfection, priority, or effect of perfection or non-perfection.

\"**Unrestricted Subsidiary**\" means any Subsidiary of the Borrower designated by the Board of Directors of the Borrower as an \"Unrestricted Subsidiary\" pursuant to a resolution of the Board of Directors, which designation shall be subject to the conditions set forth in the definition of \"Unrestricted Subsidiary\" in Section 1.01 and the provisions of Section 6.06; *provided* that on the Closing Date, no Subsidiary of the Borrower shall be an Unrestricted Subsidiary.

\"**Weighted Average Life to Maturity**\" means, when applied to any Indebtedness at any date, the number of years obtained by dividing (a) the sum of the products obtained by multiplying (i) the amount of each then remaining installment, sinking fund, serial maturity, or other required payments of principal (including payment at final maturity) in respect thereof by (ii) the number of years (calculated to the nearest one-twelfth) that will elapse between such date and the making of such payment by (b) the then outstanding principal amount of such Indebtedness.

In addition to the foregoing, the following terms shall have the meanings ascribed to them in the Sections of this Agreement indicated: \"Benchmark Replacement\" (Section 1.07), \"Borrowing Request\" (Section 2.02), \"Compliance Certificate\" (Section 5.02), \"Defaulting Lender\" (Section 2.12), \"Eligible Assignee\" (Section 10.05), \"Excess Cash Flow\" (Section 2.05(c)), \"Multiemployer Plan\" (Section 4.06), \"Prepayment Event Notice\" (Section 2.05(b)(v)), \"Reportable Event\" (Section 4.06), \"Secured Parties\" (Section 8.01), \"Total Revolving Exposure\" (Section 2.01(b)), and such other terms as may be defined elsewhere in this Agreement. All other capitalized terms used herein and not otherwise defined shall have the meanings assigned to them in the UCC.

**[Section 1.02 --- Other Interpretive Provisions]{.underline}**

With reference to this Agreement and each other Loan Document, unless otherwise specified herein or in such other Loan Document:

\(a\) The definitions of terms herein shall apply equally to the singular and plural forms of the terms defined. Whenever the context may require, any pronoun shall include the corresponding masculine, feminine, and neuter forms. The words \"include,\" \"includes,\" and \"including\" shall be deemed to be followed by the phrase \"without limitation.\" The word \"will\" shall be construed to have the same meaning and effect as the word \"shall.\" Unless the context requires otherwise, (i) any definition of or reference to any agreement, instrument, or other document (including this Agreement) shall be construed as referring to such agreement, instrument, or other document as from time to time amended, restated, supplemented, or otherwise modified (subject to any restrictions on such amendments, restatements, supplements, or modifications set forth herein or in any other Loan Document), (ii) any reference herein to any Person shall be construed to include such Person\'s successors and assigns, (iii) the words \"hereto,\" \"herein,\" \"hereof,\" and \"hereunder,\" and words of similar import when used in any Loan Document, shall be construed to refer to such Loan Document in its entirety and not to any particular provision thereof, (iv) all references in a Loan Document to Articles, Sections, Exhibits, and Schedules shall be construed to refer to Articles and Sections of, and Exhibits and Schedules to, such Loan Document, (v) any reference to any law shall include all statutory and regulatory provisions consolidating, amending, replacing, or interpreting such law and any reference to any law or regulation shall, unless otherwise specified, refer to such law or regulation as amended, modified, or supplemented from time to time, and (vi) the words \"asset\" and \"property\" shall be construed to have the same meaning and effect and to refer to any and all tangible and intangible assets and properties, including cash, securities, accounts, and contract rights.

\(b\) The headings of the various Articles, Sections, Exhibits, and Schedules of this Agreement are for convenience of reference only and shall not affect the meaning, construction, or interpretation of any provision hereof.

\(c\) In the computation of periods of time from a specified date to a later specified date, the word \"from\" means \"from and including,\" the words \"to\" and \"until\" each mean \"to but excluding,\" and the word \"through\" means \"to and including.\"

\(d\) Section headings herein and the Table of Contents are included for convenience of reference only and shall not affect the interpretation of this Agreement.

\(e\) References to the \"knowledge\" of the Borrower or any Loan Party shall mean the actual knowledge, after reasonable inquiry, of any Responsible Officer of such Loan Party.

\(f\) References to \"the Borrower and its Restricted Subsidiaries\" or similar expressions shall be deemed to include successors and permitted assigns of each such Person.

**[Section 1.03 --- Accounting Terms]{.underline}**

\(a\) All accounting terms not specifically or completely defined herein shall be construed in conformity with, and all financial data (including financial ratios and other financial calculations) required to be submitted pursuant to this Agreement shall be prepared in conformity with, GAAP applied on a consistent basis, as in effect from time to time, applied in a manner consistent with that used in preparing the financial statements referred to in Section 4.04, except as otherwise specifically prescribed herein.

\(b\) If at any time any change in GAAP would affect the computation of any financial ratio or requirement set forth in any Loan Document, and either the Borrower or the Required Lenders shall so request, the Administrative Agent, the Lenders, and the Borrower shall negotiate in good faith to amend such ratio or requirement to preserve the original intent thereof in light of such change in GAAP (subject to the approval of the Required Lenders); *provided* that, until so amended, (i) such ratio or requirement shall continue to be computed in accordance with GAAP prior to such change therein and (ii) the Borrower shall provide to the Administrative Agent and the Lenders financial statements and other documents required under this Agreement or as reasonably requested hereunder setting forth a reconciliation between calculations of such ratio or requirement made before and after giving effect to such change in GAAP (the \"**Frozen GAAP**\" provision).

\(c\) Notwithstanding anything to the contrary herein, for purposes of determining compliance with any test or covenant contained in this Agreement with respect to any period during which any Permitted Acquisition or other acquisition or any disposition of a material business unit, line of business, or division has been consummated, Consolidated EBITDA and Consolidated Net Income shall be calculated on a Pro Forma Basis.

**[Section 1.04 --- Rounding]{.underline}**

Any financial ratios required to be maintained by the Borrower pursuant to this Agreement shall be calculated by dividing the appropriate component by the other component, carrying the result to one place more than the number of places by which such ratio is expressed herein, and rounding the result up or down to the nearest number (with a rounding-up if there is no nearest number). All financial ratios shall be tested to two decimal places.

**[Section 1.05 --- References to Agreements, Laws, etc.]{.underline}**

Unless otherwise specified, (a) all references herein to agreements and other contractual instruments shall be deemed to include all subsequent amendments, restatements, extensions, supplements, and other modifications thereto, but only to the extent that such amendments, restatements, extensions, supplements, and other modifications are not prohibited by any Loan Document, and (b) all references to any law shall include all statutory and regulatory provisions consolidating, amending, replacing, supplementing, or interpreting such law and any reference to any law or regulation shall, unless otherwise specified, refer to such law or regulation as amended, modified, or supplemented from time to time.

**[Section 1.06 --- Times of Day]{.underline}**

Unless otherwise specified, all references herein to times of day shall be references to Eastern Time (daylight or standard, as applicable).

**[Section 1.07 --- Rates; SOFR Notification]{.underline}**

\(a\) **Benchmark Replacement.** Notwithstanding anything to the contrary herein or in any other Loan Document, if a Benchmark Transition Event and its related Benchmark Replacement Date have occurred prior to any setting of the then-current Benchmark, then:

> \(i\) If a Benchmark Replacement is determined in connection with a Benchmark Transition Event, such Benchmark Replacement will replace such Benchmark for all purposes hereunder and under any Loan Document in respect of any Benchmark setting at or after 5:00 p.m. (New York City time) on the fifth Business Day after the date notice of such Benchmark Replacement is provided to the Lenders, without any amendment to, or further action or consent of any other party to, this Agreement or any other Loan Document, so long as the Administrative Agent has not received, by such time, written notice of objection to such Benchmark Replacement from Lenders comprising the Required Lenders.
>
> \(ii\) The Administrative Agent will promptly notify the Borrower and the Lenders of (A) any occurrence of a Benchmark Transition Event, (B) the implementation of any Benchmark Replacement, (C) the effectiveness of any Benchmark Replacement Conforming Changes, (D) the removal or reinstatement of any tenor of a Benchmark pursuant hereto, and (E) the commencement or conclusion of any Benchmark Unavailability Period. Any determination, decision, or election that may be made by the Administrative Agent or, if specified, by any Lender (or group of Lenders) pursuant to this Section 1.07, including any determination with respect to a tenor, rate, or adjustment or of the occurrence or non-occurrence of an event, circumstance, or date and any decision to take or refrain from taking any action or any selection, will be conclusive and binding absent manifest error and may be made in its or their sole discretion and without consent from any other party to this Agreement or any other Loan Document, except, in each case, as expressly required pursuant to this Section 1.07.

\(b\) **Inability to Determine Rates.** If the Administrative Agent determines (which determination shall be conclusive absent manifest error) that (i) adequate and reasonable means do not exist for ascertaining the Adjusted Term SOFR for any requested Interest Period, or (ii) the applicable Adjusted Term SOFR for any requested Interest Period does not adequately and fairly reflect the cost to such Lenders of making or maintaining such Loans, then the Administrative Agent shall give notice thereof to the Borrower and the Lenders, whereupon (A) any Interest Election Request that requests the conversion of any Loan to, or continuation of any Loan as, a SOFR Loan shall be ineffective, and (B) if any Borrowing Request requests a SOFR Loan, such Loan shall be made as an ABR Loan; *provided* that if the circumstances giving rise to such notice no longer exist, the Administrative Agent shall so notify the Borrower and the Lenders, and such notice shall cease to be effective.

\(c\) **No Liability.** The Administrative Agent does not warrant, and shall not have any liability or responsibility for, and shall not otherwise be responsible for, the administration, submission, performance, or any other matter related to the Term SOFR Reference Rate or any component thereof, or any rate that is an alternative to or replacement of any of the foregoing, including as to whether the composition or characteristics of any such alternative, successor, or replacement rate (including any Benchmark Replacement) will be similar to, or produce the same value or economic equivalence of, or have the same volume or liquidity as, the Adjusted Term SOFR, the Term SOFR Reference Rate, or SOFR, or any other Benchmark prior to its discontinuance or unavailability.

For purposes of this Section 1.07:

\"**Benchmark**\" means, initially, the Term SOFR Reference Rate; *provided* that if a Benchmark Transition Event has occurred with respect to the Term SOFR Reference Rate or the then-current Benchmark, then \"Benchmark\" means the applicable Benchmark Replacement to the extent that such Benchmark Replacement has replaced such prior benchmark rate pursuant to Section 1.07(a).

\"**Benchmark Replacement**\" means the sum of (a) the alternate benchmark rate that has been selected by the Administrative Agent and the Borrower as the replacement for the then-current Benchmark, giving due consideration to (i) any selection or recommendation of a replacement benchmark rate or the mechanism for determining such a rate by the Relevant Governmental Body, or (ii) any evolving or then-prevailing market convention for determining a benchmark rate as a replacement for the then-current Benchmark for Dollar-denominated syndicated credit facilities at such time in the United States, and (b) the related Benchmark Replacement Adjustment; *provided* that if such Benchmark Replacement as so determined would be less than the applicable floor rate, such Benchmark Replacement will be deemed to be such floor rate for purposes of this Agreement and the other Loan Documents.

\"**Benchmark Replacement Adjustment**\" means, with respect to any replacement of the then-current Benchmark with an Unadjusted Benchmark Replacement, the spread adjustment, or method for calculating or determining such spread adjustment, (which may be a positive or negative value or zero) that has been selected by the Administrative Agent and the Borrower, giving due consideration to (a) any selection or recommendation of a spread adjustment, or method for calculating or determining such spread adjustment, for the replacement of such Benchmark with the applicable Unadjusted Benchmark Replacement by the Relevant Governmental Body, or (b) any evolving or then-prevailing market convention for determining a spread adjustment, or method for calculating or determining such spread adjustment, for the replacement of such Benchmark with the applicable Unadjusted Benchmark Replacement for Dollar-denominated syndicated credit facilities at such time.

\"**Benchmark Replacement Conforming Changes**\" means, with respect to any Benchmark Replacement, any technical, administrative, or operational changes (including changes to the definition of \"ABR,\" the definition of \"Business Day,\" the definition of \"Interest Period,\" timing and frequency of determining rates and making payments of interest, timing of borrowing requests or prepayment, conversion or continuation notices, the applicability and length of lookback periods, the applicability of breakage provisions, and other technical, administrative, or operational matters) that the Administrative Agent decides may be appropriate to reflect the adoption and implementation of such Benchmark Replacement and to permit the administration thereof by the Administrative Agent in a manner substantially consistent with market practice (or, if the Administrative Agent decides that adoption of any portion of such market practice is not administratively feasible or if the Administrative Agent determines that no market practice for the administration of such Benchmark Replacement exists, in such other manner of administration as the Administrative Agent decides is reasonably necessary in connection with the administration of this Agreement and the other Loan Documents).

\"**Benchmark Transition Event**\" means the occurrence of one or more of the following events with respect to the then-current Benchmark: (a) a public statement or publication of information by or on behalf of the administrator of such Benchmark (or the published component used in the calculation thereof) announcing that such administrator has ceased or will cease to provide all available tenors of such Benchmark (or such component thereof), permanently or indefinitely, *provided* that, at the time of such statement or publication, there is no successor administrator that will continue to provide any available tenor of such Benchmark (or such component thereof), (b) a public statement or publication of information by the regulatory supervisor for the administrator of such Benchmark (or the published component used in the calculation thereof), the Board, the Federal Reserve Bank of New York, an insolvency official with jurisdiction over the administrator for such Benchmark (or such component), a resolution authority with jurisdiction over the administrator for such Benchmark (or such component), or a court or an entity with similar insolvency or resolution authority over the administrator for such Benchmark (or such component), in each case which states that the administrator of such Benchmark (or such component) has ceased or will cease to provide all available tenors of such Benchmark (or such component thereof) permanently or indefinitely, *provided* that, at the time of such statement or publication, there is no successor administrator that will continue to provide any available tenor of such Benchmark (or such component thereof), or (c) a public statement or publication of information by the regulatory supervisor for the administrator of such Benchmark (or the published component used in the calculation thereof) announcing that all available tenors of such Benchmark (or such component thereof) are no longer, or as of a specified future date will no longer be, representative.

\"**Benchmark Replacement Date**\" means the earliest to occur of the following events with respect to the then-current Benchmark: (a) in the case of clause (a) or (b) of the definition of \"Benchmark Transition Event,\" the later of (i) the date of the public statement or publication of information referenced therein and (ii) the date on which the administrator of such Benchmark (or the published component used in the calculation thereof) permanently or indefinitely ceases to provide all available tenors of such Benchmark (or such component thereof), and (b) in the case of clause (c) of the definition of \"Benchmark Transition Event,\" the first date on which such Benchmark (or such component thereof) has been determined and announced by the regulatory supervisor for the administrator of such Benchmark (or such component thereof) to be no longer representative, *provided* that such non-representativeness will be determined by reference to the most recent statement or publication referenced in such clause (c) and even if any available tenor of such Benchmark (or such component thereof) continues to be provided on such date.

\"**Benchmark Unavailability Period**\" means the period (if any) (a) beginning at the time that a Benchmark Replacement Date has occurred if, at such time, no Benchmark Replacement has replaced the then-current Benchmark for all purposes hereunder and under any Loan Document in accordance with this Section 1.07 and (b) ending at the time that a Benchmark Replacement has replaced the then-current Benchmark for all purposes hereunder and under any Loan Document in accordance with this Section 1.07.

\"**Relevant Governmental Body**\" means the Board and/or the Federal Reserve Bank of New York, or a committee officially endorsed or convened by the Board and/or the Federal Reserve Bank of New York, or any successor thereto.

\"**Unadjusted Benchmark Replacement**\" means the applicable Benchmark Replacement excluding the related Benchmark Replacement Adjustment.

**[Section 1.08 --- Letter of Credit Amounts]{.underline}**

Unless otherwise specified herein, the amount of a Letter of Credit at any time shall be deemed to be the stated amount of such Letter of Credit at such time. The Dollar equivalent of the face amount of each Letter of Credit shall be determined (a) on the date of issuance, amendment, renewal, or extension of such Letter of Credit, and (b) on the first Business Day of each calendar month thereafter. Any fluctuation in the Dollar equivalent of any Letter of Credit shall not result in a Default if the aggregate LC Exposure at the time of issuance did not exceed the Letter of Credit Sub-Facility.

**[ARTICLE II --- THE COMMITMENTS AND BORROWINGS]{.underline}**

**[Section 2.01 --- The Loans]{.underline}**

**(a) Term Loan A.** Subject to the terms and conditions set forth herein, each Term Lender severally (and not jointly) agrees to make a term loan (each, a \"**Term Loan**\") to the Borrower on the Closing Date in a single drawing in a principal amount equal to such Lender\'s Term Loan A Commitment. The aggregate principal amount of all Term Loans on the Closing Date shall be \$350,000,000. Amounts repaid or prepaid in respect of Term Loans may not be reborrowed. The Term Loan A Commitment of each Term Lender shall terminate immediately and without further action upon the funding of such Term Lender\'s Term Loan on the Closing Date.

**(b) Revolving Loans.** Subject to the terms and conditions set forth herein, each Revolving Lender severally (and not jointly) agrees to make revolving loans (each, a \"**Revolving Loan**\") to the Borrower from time to time during the period from the Closing Date to and including the Business Day immediately preceding the Revolving Maturity Date, in an aggregate principal amount at any time outstanding not to exceed such Lender\'s Revolving Commitment *minus* such Lender\'s Pro Rata Share of the aggregate outstanding LC Exposure at such time. The aggregate amount of all Revolving Loans outstanding at any time, together with the aggregate outstanding LC Exposure, shall not exceed the aggregate Revolving Commitments then in effect (such aggregate amount, the \"**Total Revolving Exposure**\"). Within the foregoing limits, and subject to the terms, conditions, and limitations set forth herein, the Borrower may borrow, repay, and reborrow Revolving Loans. The aggregate Revolving Commitments on the Closing Date are \$125,000,000.

**[Section 2.02 --- Borrowings, Conversions, and Continuations]{.underline}**

**(a) Borrowing Requests.** Each borrowing of Loans shall be made upon the Borrower\'s irrevocable written notice to the Administrative Agent in the form of a Borrowing Request (a \"**Borrowing Request**\"), which Borrowing Request shall be substantially in the form of Exhibit C hereto (or such other form as may be approved by the Administrative Agent) and must be received by the Administrative Agent not later than (i) 12:00 noon (Eastern Time) three Business Days prior to the requested date of any borrowing of, or conversion to or continuation of, SOFR Loans and (ii) 12:00 noon (Eastern Time) one Business Day prior to the requested date of any borrowing of ABR Loans. Each Borrowing Request shall specify (A) whether the Borrower is requesting a Revolving Loan or Term Loan, (B) the aggregate amount of the requested borrowing, (C) the date of such borrowing (which shall be a Business Day), (D) whether such borrowing is to be a borrowing of SOFR Loans or ABR Loans, and (E) in the case of a borrowing of SOFR Loans, the initial Interest Period to be applicable thereto. If no election as to the Type of borrowing is specified, then the requested borrowing shall be an ABR Loan. If no Interest Period is specified with respect to any requested borrowing of SOFR Loans, then the Borrower shall be deemed to have selected an Interest Period of one month\'s duration.

**(b) Minimum Amounts.** Each borrowing of SOFR Loans shall be in a minimum principal amount of \$5,000,000 and in integral multiples of \$1,000,000 in excess thereof (or the remaining available amount of the applicable Commitment). Each borrowing of ABR Loans shall be in a minimum principal amount of \$1,000,000 and in integral multiples of \$500,000 in excess thereof (or the remaining available amount of the applicable Commitment).

**(c) Interest Period Elections.** The Borrower may elect Interest Periods of 1, 3, or 6 months\' duration (or, if agreed to by all affected Lenders, 12 months\' duration) with respect to SOFR Loans. Any such election shall be made by delivery of an Interest Election Request in accordance with Section 2.02(d).

**(d) Conversion and Continuation.** The Borrower may elect from time to time to convert SOFR Loans to ABR Loans by giving the Administrative Agent at least one Business Day\'s prior irrevocable written notice of such election, and the Borrower may elect from time to time to convert ABR Loans to SOFR Loans, or to continue SOFR Loans for an additional Interest Period, by giving the Administrative Agent at least three Business Days\' prior irrevocable written notice of such election, in each case by delivery of an Interest Election Request substantially in the form of Exhibit D hereto (or such other form as may be approved by the Administrative Agent). Each such Interest Election Request shall specify (i) the Loans to which such Interest Election Request applies and, if different options are being elected with respect to different portions thereof, the portions thereof to be allocated to each resulting borrowing (in which case the information to be specified pursuant to clauses (ii) through (iv) below shall be specified for each resulting borrowing), (ii) the effective date of the election made pursuant to such Interest Election Request (which shall be a Business Day), (iii) whether the resulting borrowing is to be a SOFR Loan or ABR Loan, and (iv) if the resulting borrowing is a SOFR Loan, the Interest Period to be applicable thereto. If any such Interest Election Request requests a SOFR Loan but does not specify an Interest Period, then the Borrower shall be deemed to have selected an Interest Period of one month\'s duration.

**(e) Administrative Agent Actions.** Promptly following receipt of a Borrowing Request or an Interest Election Request, the Administrative Agent shall advise each applicable Lender of the details thereof and of the amount of such Lender\'s Loan to be made or its participation in the relevant conversion or continuation.

**(f) Funding by Lenders.** Unless the Administrative Agent shall have received notice from a Lender prior to the proposed date of any borrowing that such Lender will not make available to the Administrative Agent such Lender\'s share of such borrowing, the Administrative Agent may assume that such Lender has made such share available on such date in accordance with this Section 2.02 and may, in reliance upon such assumption, make available to the Borrower a corresponding amount. In such event, if a Lender has not in fact made its share of the applicable borrowing available to the Administrative Agent, then the applicable Lender and the Borrower severally agree to pay to the Administrative Agent forthwith on demand such corresponding amount in immediately available funds with interest thereon, for each day from and including the date such amount is made available to the Borrower to but excluding the date of payment to the Administrative Agent, at (i) in the case of a payment to be made by such Lender, the greater of the Federal Funds Rate and a rate determined by the Administrative Agent in accordance with banking industry rules on interbank compensation, and (ii) in the case of a payment to be made by the Borrower, the interest rate applicable to ABR Loans.

**[Section 2.03 --- Incremental Facility]{.underline}**
